title: AI Ethics and Safety
description: The capstone of AI Foundations: how AI systems can encode bias, threaten privacy, resist explanation, enable misuse, and raise open questions of accountability and long-term safety.
objectives: Explain how a model trained on biased or unrepresentative data can reproduce and amplify that bias, using a concrete hiring-screening example
Describe the privacy risks of models trained on personal data, including memorisation and inference of sensitive information
Explain the black-box problem and why explainability matters for high-stakes decisions such as medical diagnosis and loan approval
Identify potential misuses of generative AI, such as deepfakes and automated misinformation at scale
Discuss who can reasonably bear responsibility when an AI system causes harm
Give a balanced overview of AI safety and alignment concerns for more capable future systems
